Off Calendar Jul-06-2010 Continued To Aug-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Connolly for Wells Fargo Bank.

Ms. Connolly stated that because it appears that the Trust is subject to private foundation rules it is required to make yearly distributions to the Beneficiaries, that no distributions were made from the Trust for 2007 or 2008, that they needed to go back and do a income reconciliation accounting to provide to the Beneficiaries, that they need to see if the Beneficiaries are entitled to any further net income from the Trust and requested time to do so. The matter was continued to August 3, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Wall (Counsel for the American Humane Society, Objector); Stephen Gould (Counsel for Wells Fargo Bank, Trustee).

Counsel represented to the Court that they had been in contact with the Presiding Judge's department to secure a trial date but had not yet agreed on what that date would be inasmuch as it had not yet been determined what issues would be addressed at trial. Counsel had agreed, however, that the 2006 Accounting would be included as a trial issue. A Stipulation and Order were presented to the Court to that effect and the Court signed the same in open court. Since the matter has been referred to the Presiding Judge, no further hearing date was set.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Petition For Damages Resulting From Acts Of Professional Negligence; Breach Of Trust For Violation Of Probate Code Sec. 16047 (C); Violation Of The Duty Loyalty; And Objections To Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-20-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Stephen Gould appearing for trustee, Wells Fargo Bank. John Granucci appearing for SF SPCA. Richard Wall appearing for American Humane Society and Marin Humane Society. Discovery issue is resolved. Mr. Gould wants to have the matter set for trial in Probate Court. Mr. Wall thinks the trial will take three days. He wants to have this matter sent out for arbitration. Mr. Gould objects to arbitration. He wants the Court to make a ruling. Matter is referred to the Presiding Judge to set for trial. Order signed.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR# 7782. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: J. Peralta.

Off Calendar Feb-05-2007 Continued To Feb-20-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Damages Resulting From Acts Of Professional Negligence; Breach Of Trust For Violation Of Probate Code Sec. 16047 (C); Violation Of The Duty Loyalty; And Objections To Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-21-2006 Continued To Feb-05-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: John Granucci appearing for SPCA. Stephen Gould appearing for trustee Wells Fargo Bank. Richard Wall appearing for Marin Humane Society. Mr. Granucci stated that the SPCA does have an endowment fund. He has filed a declaration of facts and issues. Mr. Wall wants to have this matter set for trial. Trial will take three days. He wants to question the performance of this trust. Mr. Gould does not agree to the length of the trial and the scope of the hearing based on Commissioner McMath's prior order. Court will either have this matter referred to Commissioner McMath for clarification of her prior order or to have this matter referred to the Presiding Judge for trial setting. Matter submitted. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Petition For Damages Resulting From Acts Of Professional Negligence; Breach Of Trust For Violation Of Probate Code Sec. 16047 (C); Violation Of The Duty Loyalty; And Objections To Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-08-2006 Continued To Dec-13-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Wall (Counsel for the American Humane Society - bebficiary); John Granucci (Counsel for the San Francisco SPCA - beneficiary); Stephen Gould (Counsel for Wells Fargo Bank, Trustee).

With a view toward ascertaining the appropriate venue for and length of trial in this matter, counsel presented their contentions as to the issues and scope of discovery remaining. After discussion, the Court ordered Mr. Wall and Mr. Granucci to prepare and file a Declaration(s) re: facts to be proved at trial and to serve Mr. Gould with the same by personal service on or before December 6, 2006 with continued hearing on the same on December 13, 2006 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Trudy Verzosa.

Off Calendar May-23-2006 Continued To Jun-28-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Stephen Gould appearing for trustee, Wells Fargo Bank. John Granucci and Richard Wall appearing for American Humane Society, SF Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals and Marin Humane Society. Discovery issue argued by both sides. Discovery motion is set for hearing on 6/6. Commissioner McMath's ruling covers some of the discovery request. Mr. Granucci requests for a trial date on his petition. Trial will take 2 to 5 days. Discovery deadline is 6/20. Both petitions are continued to 6/28 for status. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-23-2006 Continued To Jun-28-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-18-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Gould (Counsel for Moving Party Wells Fargo Bank); Richard Wall (Counsel for Respondents American Humane Society, San Francisco Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Marin Humane Society); John Granucci (Of Counsel, Respondents American Humane Society, San Francisco Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Marin Humane Society).

At the outset of the hearing, the Court announced its tentative ruling; to wit: to grant the Motion to Strike and the Motion in Limine. After extensive argument by Mr. Granucci concerning the legal authority with respect to the res judicata effect of Probate Court Orders approving accountings (and the factual differences in the case at bar) and rebuttal by Mr. Gould, the Court adopted its tentative ruling and granted Wells Fargo's Motion to Strike and its related Motion in Limine.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Bob Balian CSR# 5772; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

Motion In Limine
Off Calendar May-18-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Gould (Counsel for Moving Party Wells Fargo Bank); Richard Wall (Counsel for Respondents American Humane Society, San Francisco Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Marin Humane Society); John Granucci (Of Counsel, Respondents American Humane Society, San Francisco Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Marin Humane Society).

At the outset of the hearing, the Court announced its tentative ruling; to wit: to grant the Motion to Strike and the Motion in Limine. After extensive argument by Mr. Granucci concerning the legal authority with respect to the res judicata effect of Probate Court Orders approving accountings (and the factual differences in the case at bar) and rebuttal by Mr. Gould, the Court adopted its tentative ruling and granted Wells Fargo's Motion to Strike and its related Motion in Limine.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Bob Balian CSR# 5772; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
